commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=f40e41204d81c109e385857b6a9c2ac5afe56e65)
commands.review_summary:
Adds a node-pairing auth-rate-limit scope, wraps WebSocket node-pairing request creation in the auth limiter, preserves paired reconnects when upgrade pairing is throttled, and adds gateway regression tests.

PR surface: Source +72, Tests +186. Total +258 across 5 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main calls `requestNodePairing` from WebSocket node connect without a limiter, and that function enters a serialized storage lock. I did not run live proof because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Needs contributor real behavior proof plus gateway/security owner judgment on the auth-rate-limit compatibility policy; that is not a narrow safe automation repair.

target.title: Rate limit node pairing requests [AI]
target.body:
## Summary

- Adds a gateway auth-rate-limit scope for node-role pairing requests created during WebSocket connect.
- Runs first-time node pairing requests through the existing serialized per-IP limiter before they enter the node-pairing storage lock.
- Returns the existing structured rate-limit connect error when the pairing request bucket is exhausted.
- Adds regression coverage for concurrent first-time node connects so only the configured number reach pending node-pairing state.

AI-assisted: yes.

## Linked context

No public issue is linked in this PR body.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: concurrent first-time node connection attempts are throttled before they can queue node-pairing storage writes.
- Real environment tested: local source checkout with the gateway Vitest harness.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `pnpm format -- src/gateway/auth-rate-limit.ts src/gateway/server/ws-connection/message-handler.ts src/gateway/server.node-pairing-rate-limit.test.ts`; `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server.node-pairing-rate-limit.test.ts src/gateway/server.preauth-bootstrap-token-rate-limit.test.ts src/gateway/auth-rate-limit.test.ts`; `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/gateway/auth-rate-limit.ts src/gateway/server/ws-connection/message-handler.ts src/gateway/server.node-pairing-rate-limit.test.ts`; `git diff --check`.
- Evidence after fix: focused Vitest shard passed 8 files / 102 tests, including concurrent node-pairing rate-limit coverage.
- Observed result after fix: with `maxAttempts: 3`, eight concurrent first-time node connects create three pending node-pairing requests and return five structured auth rate-limit responses.
- What was not tested: live non-loopback gateway traffic and GitHub Actions CI are pending.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: no external device or remote network environment was used.
- Before evidence: the affected connect path called `requestNodePairing` directly without a node-pairing rate-limit scope.

## Tests and validation

Commands run:

- `pnpm format -- src/gateway/auth-rate-limit.ts src/gateway/server/ws-connection/message-handler.ts src/gateway/server.node-pairing-rate-limit.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server.node-pairing-rate-limit.test.ts src/gateway/server.preauth-bootstrap-token-rate-limit.test.ts src/gateway/auth-rate-limit.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/gateway/auth-rate-limit.ts src/gateway/server/ws-connection/message-handler.ts src/gateway/server.node-pairing-rate-limit.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`

Regression coverage added: `src/gateway/server.node-pairing-rate-limit.test.ts` covers concurrent first-time node-pairing attempts through the real WebSocket connect path.

What failed before this fix: the node connect path had no node-pairing limiter before `requestNodePairing` entered the shared storage lock.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes. Repeated first-time node pairing attempts from the same rate-limit identity can now receive the existing structured rate-limit connect error when the configured bucket is exhausted.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No. This reuses the existing gateway auth rate-limit configuration and defaults.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes. This changes the gateway security/auth boundary for node-role pairing requests by adding per-IP throttling before node-pairing storage writes.

What is the highest-risk area?

Legitimate node reconnect loops could hit the node-pairing bucket while waiting for operator approval.

How is that risk mitigated?

The bucket uses the existing gateway rate-limit configuration, loopback exemption behavior, browser-origin limiter selection, and serialized attempt helper already used by auth-sensitive connect paths.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

Wait for review gates, advisory validation, and GitHub Actions CI.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

GitHub Actions CI and automated review gates are pending.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

No PR reviewer comments have been received yet.
